title: Admin approval dashboard
description: Build an admin-only page (admin.html) where Pete can see all pending signup requests with Google name, email, profile photo, and signup date. Approve and Reject buttons per user. Also show approved and suspended users with ability to suspend/reinstate. Page must be protected — only profiles with is_admin=true can access it; anyone else gets redirected. Blocked on: DB schema bead, sign-in bead.

title: Sign-in page with Google button and sign-out flow
description: Create or update the login/landing page with a single 'Sign in with Google' button using Supabase Auth. After OAuth callback: if status=approved, redirect to view.html. If status=pending, show a 'Your request is pending approval' holding page. If status=rejected/suspended, show appropriate message. Add sign-out button to all authenticated pages. Handle the OAuth callback URL. Blocked on: Google OAuth infra bead, JWT session bead.

title: Replace api_key auth with Supabase JWT sessions
description: The app currently authenticates via api_key stored in localStorage (fw_profile_id). Replace with real Supabase Auth sessions: supabase.auth.getUser() instead of localStorage, pass JWT bearer tokens to manage-places Edge Function instead of api_key user_token, update Edge Function to verify JWT via Supabase Auth rather than profiles.api_key lookup. Touches every page (view.html, add-place.html, edit-places.html, profile.html) and the Edge Function. Blocked on: Google OAuth infrastructure bead.

title: DB: approval_status + admin flag on profiles
description: Add approval_status column to profiles table (values: pending, approved, rejected, suspended) defaulting to 'pending'. Add is_admin boolean column. Update RLS policies so only 'approved' users can read/write places. Add created_at to profiles if missing. Migration must be run manually in Supabase SQL Editor. Designate Pete (pmorse@gmail.com) as the initial admin via UPDATE.

title: Enable Google OAuth via Supabase Auth
description: Configure Supabase Auth to use Google as the only sign-in provider. Steps: create OAuth credentials in Google Cloud Console (client ID + secret), enable Google provider in Supabase Auth dashboard, set redirect URLs. No code changes yet — this is the infrastructure prerequisite that all auth beads depend on. Deliverable: Google OAuth working in Supabase dashboard, redirect URL configured for both localhost and GitHub Pages.

title: Share links viewable without registration
description: When a place is shared via link (view.html?place=<id>), the recipient must be able to view it without being a registered Five Wonders user. Currently an access code is required. The get_public Edge Function action exists but the frontend may redirect unauthenticated visitors to login before rendering the map/sheet. Need to verify and fix the full recipient flow so a non-member can open a share link and see the place popup with no login wall.
close_reason: Guest share-link flow fixed: non-registered users go straight to get_public, error alert suppressed for anon, guest banner added, RLS migration for anon public reads.

title: Error loading places in edit view
description: After deploying the is_private feature (five-wonders-re2), the edit-places page shows 'Error loading places' when loading the list or selecting an individual place to edit. Likely caused by the edge function or view returning the new is_private column in a way the client doesn't expect, or a Supabase schema cache issue with the updated view.
close_reason: View places_with_profiles was missing is_private column. Added in migration 007. View now includes all columns needed by the edge function.

title: Fix SECURITY DEFINER on places_with_profiles view
description: Supabase Advisor flagged public.places_with_profiles as a CRITICAL security issue: the view is defined with the SECURITY DEFINER property, which runs with the privileges of the view creator rather than the querying user. This can bypass RLS policies. Need to recreate the view without SECURITY DEFINER (or explicitly as SECURITY INVOKER). Must verify the 5W app still works after the change since this view is likely used in place listings.
close_reason: Created migration 005_fix_view_security.sql to recreate places_with_profiles with security_invoker=true

title: Enable RLS on public.spatial_ref_sys
description: Supabase Advisor flagged public.spatial_ref_sys as a CRITICAL security issue: RLS is disabled on this public table. Need to enable Row Level Security on this table. Must verify the 5W app still works after the change since spatial_ref_sys is a PostGIS system table — enabling RLS on it could break geometry/geography queries if not handled carefully.
close_reason: Not actionable: spatial_ref_sys is a PostGIS system table owned by supabase_admin. Cannot enable RLS (permission denied) and cannot move to extensions schema because places.location column depends on the geography type. This is a known Supabase limitation — the Advisor warning cannot be resolved while PostGIS is in use in the public schema.

title: List count on profile page
description: Add a 'Lists' stat to profile.html alongside the existing Wonders count — e.g. '12 Wonders · 3 Lists'. Tapping the Lists count could link to a page or filter showing the user's lists. Requires querying the lists table for count where user_id matches current user.

title: Show lists on place detail view
description: When viewing a place (in the map sheet or popup), show a section listing which lists include that place. Show all public lists + any private lists where the current user is the owner. Each list name is a tappable link that navigates to the list detail page. If the place is not in any lists, the section is hidden. This requires a query joining place_id through list_places to lists.

title: Share a list
description: Add a share button to the list detail page (and list management UI). For public lists, tapping share opens the native iOS/Android share sheet (navigator.share) with the list title, description, and list URL pre-filled — same pattern as sharing a single place. For private lists, show 'This list is private and cannot be shared.' message. List URL should be the direct list detail page URL.

title: List detail page: map + list/map toggle
description: When a user opens a list URL (view.html?list=<id> or list.html?id=<id>), show all places in the list as pins on a map. Yelp-style toggle button switches between map view (pins) and list view (scrollable cards). Tapping a place in list view flies to it on the map and opens the place sheet/popup. Header shows list title and description. For private lists, only the owner sees them; others get a 'private list' message. For public lists, anyone (including non-registered users) can view.

title: Profile page: Google account info, ToS, account deletion
description: Update profile.html to show: Google profile photo, name, and email pulled from Supabase Auth session. Account status badge (pending/approved/suspended). Sign-out button. Terms of Service acceptance checkbox shown on first login (must accept before request is submitted to admin). Account deletion request button — deletes all user places and profile, signs out. Blocked on: JWT session bead, sign-in bead.

title: Existing user migration to Google OAuth
description: Current users authenticated via api_key in localStorage need to link their Google account to their existing profile without losing their saved places. Flow: user signs in with Google, system matches by email to existing profile, merges accounts, preserves all places. If no match, treat as new signup (pending). One-time migration — after all users have migrated, api_key column can be deprecated. Blocked on: sign-in bead.

title: Email notifications for signup approval workflow
description: Send email via Supabase Edge Function + Resend (or similar) when: (1) new user requests access — email Pete with user name, email, Google photo, and an approve link; (2) user is approved — email them a welcome message with link to the app; (3) user is rejected — email them a polite decline. Store email provider API key as Supabase secret. Blocked on: DB schema bead, admin dashboard bead.

title: Rich link previews for shared places
description: When a place is shared via text/iMessage/WhatsApp, the link preview should show the place title, location (city/state), and a photo thumbnail (or default logo). GitHub Pages is static so og:title/og:image meta tags can't be set dynamically. Solution: create a new Supabase Edge Function (share-place) that fetches the place, returns a tiny HTML page with og:title, og:description, og:image, og:url, and a JS redirect to view.html?place=<id>. The share URL becomes the Edge Function URL instead of the view.html URL. og:image uses the first photo thumbnail from Supabase Storage if available, otherwise a default logo URL. Update the share handler in view.html to copy the Edge Function URL.
close_reason: share-place Edge Function deployed; og:title/og:description/og:image set from place data; share handler updated to copy Edge Function URL.

title: Redesign map place popup/sheet
description: Redesign the place bottom sheet to match Apple Maps iPhone aesthetic based on provided screenshots. Key requirements: (1) Three snap states: peek (title bar only), half-screen default, full-screen expanded. Swipe up/down to move between states. (2) Header: pill handle centered top, share icon top-left, X close top-right, bold title, gray category subtitle below. (3) Action button row: blue rounded pill for Directions (primary), gray pills for Website/Call/Edit — each with icon on top, label below. (4) Clean info rows for address, phone. (5) Horizontal scrolling photo strip. (6) Notes/'Why it's a Wonder' section. (7) 32px bottom padding. (8) All hyperlinks blue. (9) Desktop popup follows same card design language (no sheet, just styled popup). Share icon must work from sheet header.
close_reason: Apple Maps-style sheet redesign complete: new header with share/close buttons, action button row, info rows, photo strip, notes section. CSS snap states (peek/half/full). Desktop popup updated too.

title: Share a place via link
description: Users should be able to share a saved place (restaurant, attraction, etc.) by sending a link via text or any messaging app. The recipient clicks the link and lands on a 5W detail page for that place. The share link should only work if the place is public. A place is public by default. Users can check a 'Private' checkbox on their listing to prevent sharing — private places return a not-found or access-denied page when accessed via share link.
close_reason: Share link button in edit form copies view.html?place=<id>. Recipients land on the map with the place highlighted and detail sheet open. Private places show a warning before sharing and are blocked on the public fetch.

title: Create a list
description: Core list infrastructure: DB schema (lists table with id, user_id, title, description, is_private boolean defaulting false, created_at, updated_at; list_places join table with list_id, place_id, added_at). UI to create a new list, add/remove places from a list, edit list name/description, toggle public/private, and delete a list with confirmation. This is the foundation all other list beads depend on.

title: Public vs private listings
description: Within my account, I want individual places to be marked public or private. For me, I can see all. For other accounts, they can only see those I marked public. This attribute needs to be viewable by me for all listings when in the app. For starters, make all listings public.
close_reason: Added is_private boolean column (default false=public). Checkbox in edit form for desktop and mobile. Edge function updated to read/write the field.

title: CAPTCHA on sign-in page
description: Add Cloudflare Turnstile (free, privacy-friendly) to the sign-in page as an extra spam layer before the Google OAuth flow begins. Note: Google OAuth itself is strong spam protection since it requires a real Google account. Turnstile is belt-and-suspenders. Configure site key in Cloudflare dashboard, verify token server-side in the Edge Function before creating a pending profile. Blocked on: sign-in bead.
